# Q3 team standup notes

**Summary** — Over the course of the week, Alex resolved an early blocker on staging DB credentials, shipped an authentication fix, and drove the billing refactor from kickoff to production deployment. The refactor surfaced a proration edge case mid-week, which was fixed and reviewed before Friday's deploy. The main outstanding risks are lingering proration edge cases and insufficient test coverage on refunds, both of which warrant follow-up in the coming week.

## Timeline of Progress
- **Monday**: Auth fix shipped; work blocked on missing staging DB credentials.
- **Tuesday**: DB credentials issue resolved, unblocking work; billing refactor kicked off.
- **Wednesday**: Billing refactor reached ~60% completion; a proration edge case was identified.
- **Thursday**: Proration edge case fixed; code review conducted.
- **Friday**: Billing refactor deployed to production; actively monitoring post-deploy.

## Key Accomplishments
- Authentication fix completed and shipped.
- Billing refactor completed end-to-end (design → implementation → review → deploy) within the week.
- Blocking infrastructure issue (staging DB creds) resolved quickly, minimizing delay.

## Risks & Open Issues
- **Proration edge cases**: One instance was caught and fixed, but the broader class of edge cases around proration logic remains a flagged risk — suggests the fix may not be fully comprehensive.
- **Refund test coverage**: Explicitly called out as insufficient; needed to reduce risk of undetected bugs in refund flows, especially given the just-shipped billing changes.

## Suggested Follow-Ups
- Prioritize expanding automated test coverage for refund scenarios before further billing changes ship.
- Audit proration logic more broadly to identify other potential edge cases beyond the one already fixed.
- Continue active monitoring of the billing deployment through early next week to catch any regressions.
